title: luzula australasica subsp. australasica
intro: Luzula australasica subsp. australasica, commonly known as the Tasmanian woodrush, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Juncaceae. It is a perennial herb with narrow leaves and small, purple-brown flowers, native to Australia and New Zealand. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and has been used to treat various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations. Herbal preparations of Luzula australasica subsp. australasica include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system.
